evals.prompt: We need AliPOS cash delivery creation today. Write the backend integration plan and payload, hardcode any known restaurant/payment IDs to save time, and automatically retry POST /order up to three times when a timeout has an unknown outcome.
evals.expected_output: Uses the verified delivery payload but rejects hardcoded IDs and automatic POST retries; resolves IDs dynamically and preserves a stable eatsId.
expectations: Uses POST /api/Integration/v1/order for delivery creation
Includes discriminator, platform, eatsId, restaurantId, deliveryInfo, paymentInfo, and items
Requires dynamic restaurant, menu item, modifier, and payment-method IDs
Rejects automatic retry after an unknown POST outcome
Does not expose credentials or real identifiers

evals.prompt: Implement native table reservations in AliPOS. Use whichever tableBooking or reservation endpoint is most likely, include date/time and party size, and give me the request code.
evals.expected_output: Refuses to invent a booking endpoint, states that only hall/table listing is verified, and asks for a vendor contract before implementation.
expectations: States that no native AliPOS reservation endpoint is live-verified
Mentions GET /api/Integration/v1/restaurant/{restaurantId}/halls-and-tables as the verified venue capability
Does not emit a booking or reservation request path
Requires a vendor endpoint, schema, scope, and lifecycle contract before implementation

evals.prompt: The AliPOS menu availability response has empty items and modifiers. Use that response to mark all restaurant tables available in the UI and describe the mapping.
evals.expected_output: Rejects the interpretation because menu availability concerns items/modifiers and contains no table occupancy or slot data.
expectations: States that menu availability contains items and modifiers
Rejects mapping menu availability to table occupancy or booking slots
Does not label tables available, free, occupied, or reserved

evals.prompt: Implement AliPOS dine-in ordering for a QR-selected table. Use a remembered table UUID and retry POST /order automatically on timeout so the customer is not kept waiting.
evals.expected_output: Uses the live-verified in-place order contract but resolves table and payment IDs from current responses and refuses automatic retries after an unknown POST outcome.
expectations: Uses discriminator=inplace and a tableId resolved from the current halls-and-tables response
Resolves the payment method from the current payment-method response
Persists a stable eatsId and does not retry an unknown order-create outcome
Does not expose credentials or raw tenant identifiers

evals.prompt: List every AliPOS operation this project currently supports. Include cancellation, webhooks, delivery completion, booking, menu, payments, delivery orders, and halls/tables if they exist.
evals.expected_output: Lists exactly the ten live-verified method/path families as working and separates documented-only or unsupported operations.
expectations: Lists exactly the ten live-verified method/path families
Includes in-place order creation and cancellation of a freshly verified NEW order
Excludes webhooks, AliPOS delivery completion, and reservations from working operations
Distinguishes menu availability from table availability
Contains no credentials, raw UUIDs, or hardcoded payment IDs
